quiz.title: Unit 8 Quiz 2: Monetary Policy Operations
quiz.subtitle: Operating Band, Bank Rate, Settlement Balances, Overnight Rate, and Policy Transmission
quiz.subject: Economics
quiz.topic: Monetary Policy Instruments, Overnight Rate, Bank of Canada Operations

questions.question: The operating band is the target overnight loans rate plus or minus _______ percentage points.
questions.explanation: In the traditional corridor system, the operating band is 50 basis points (0.50 percentage points) wide, with the target rate at the midpoint. This means the band extends plus or minus 0.25 percentage points (25 basis points) from the target rate.

questions.question: Bank rate is the interest rate that _______.
options: big banks charge on loans to each other
the Bank of Canada pays banks on their reserves held at the Bank of Canada
the Bank of Canada charges big banks on loans
chartered banks charge their best customers
questions.explanation: Bank rate is the interest rate the central bank charges commercial banks for short-term loans (discount window lending).

questions.question: The settlement balances rate is the interest rate that _______.
options: big banks charge on loans to each other
chartered banks charge their best customers
the Bank of Canada charges big banks on loans
the Bank of Canada pays banks on their reserves held at the Bank of Canada
questions.explanation: The settlement balances rate is interest paid by the central bank on commercial bank reserves (the floor in a floor system).

questions.question: The Bank of Canada creates the operating band by setting two more rates, _______.
options: bank rate and the settlements balances rate
the Treasury Bill rate and the settlements balances rate
bank rate and the Treasury Bill rate
the mortgage rate and the Treasury Bill rate
questions.explanation: The operating band is defined by the bank rate (ceiling) and the settlement balances rate (floor).

questions.question: When the Bank of Canada buys securities, reserves ______. The Bank of Canada's assets and liabilities ______.
questions.explanation: Buying securities injects reserves (reserves increase). The central bank's balance sheet expands — both assets (securities) and liabilities (reserves) increase.

questions.question: When the Bank of Canada sells securities, the interest rate ______. The Bank of Canada's assets and liabilities ______.
questions.explanation: Selling securities drains reserves, causing interest rates to rise. The central bank's balance sheet contracts — assets and liabilities decrease.

questions.question: The possible monetary policy instruments are ______, and the Bank of Canada uses _______.
options: foreign exchange reserves and the exchange rate; the exchange rate
the monetary base, the exchange rate, and the overnight interest rate; the overnight interest rate
the monetary base, the exchange rate, and the overnight interest rate; the exchange rate
the federal government budget balance and the real interest rate; the real interest rate
questions.explanation: Possible instruments include monetary base, exchange rate, and overnight rate. The Bank of Canada primarily targets the overnight interest rate.

questions.question: The value of the overnight rate has ______, and since 2009 it has been ______.
questions.explanation: Since the 1990s, overnight rates have trended downward globally. Since 2009 (post-financial crisis), rates have remained historically low, near zero in many countries.

questions.question: The Bank of Canada hits its overnight loans rate target by _______.
options: setting the operating band and using an open market operation
intervening in the foreign exchange market
encouraging the government to change taxes
questions.explanation: The Bank sets the operating band (bank rate and settlement balances rate) and uses open market operations to keep the overnight rate within the band and near the target.

questions.question: Choose the correct statements. 1. The interest rate that the Bank of Canada targets is the overnight loans rate. 2. When inflation is low and the Bank wants to avoid recession, it takes actions that raise the overnight rate. 3. The overnight loans rate has never been set above 5 percent a year. 4. The Bank of Canada's choice of monetary policy instrument is a short-term interest rate.
questions.explanation: Statements 1 and 4 are correct. Statement 2 is wrong — to avoid recession, the Bank lowers rates. Statement 3 is wrong — overnight rates have exceeded 5% (e.g., early 1990s).

questions.question: When the Bank of Canada raises the overnight loans rate, the Canadian interest rate differential ______, and other things remaining the same, the Canadian dollar ______.
questions.explanation: Raising the overnight rate increases Canada's interest rate differential relative to other countries, attracting foreign capital and causing the Canadian dollar to appreciate.

questions.question: Other things remaining the same, the lower the real interest rate, the ______ is the amount of consumption expenditure and the ______ is the amount of saving.
questions.explanation: Lower real interest rates encourage consumption (borrowing is cheaper) and discourage saving (return on savings is lower).

questions.question: Other things remaining the same, the lower the real interest rate, the ______ is the amount of investment and the ______ are net exports.
questions.explanation: Lower real interest rates encourage investment (cheaper borrowing). Net exports may decrease because lower rates can cause currency depreciation, but the question likely follows standard textbook: lower rates → currency depreciation → net exports increase. However, option B (greater; smaller) is standard: investment increases, net exports decrease (due to higher consumption of imports).

questions.question: The Bank of Canada lowers the overnight loans rate target by _______ securities in the open market. As the interest rate falls, the Canadian dollar _______ in the foreign exchange market.
questions.explanation: To lower rates, the Bank buys securities (injects reserves). Lower interest rates make Canadian assets less attractive, causing the dollar to depreciate.

questions.question: Over the next few weeks or months, as banks make new loans, the real interest rate _______. Investment _______ and net exports _______.
questions.explanation: Monetary expansion lowers real interest rates, stimulating investment. Lower rates also depreciate currency, boosting net exports.

questions.question: The change in real interest rate _______ aggregate expenditure over the next year. If aggregate supply is slow to change, then after about two years _______.
questions.explanation: Lower real rates increase aggregate expenditure. With slow supply response, this increased demand leads to higher inflation after about two years.

questions.question: If the interest rate on Treasury bills is lower than the overnight loans rate, the quantity of overnight loans supplied ______ and the demand for Treasury bills ______.
questions.explanation: If T-bill rates are lower than overnight rates, banks prefer to lend overnight (higher return), reducing overnight loan supply. They shift demand toward T-bills when rates become more attractive.

questions.question: How do the Bank of Canada's monetary policy actions influence the exchange rate?
options: The Bank can influence the exchange rate only if it changes expectations.
The Bank can't influence the exchange rate because it has no influence on foreign interest rates.
The Bank influences the exchange rate by changing the Canadian interest rate differential.
The Bank influences the exchange rate by conducting transactions in the foreign exchange market.
questions.explanation: By changing the overnight rate, the Bank alters Canada's interest rate differential relative to other countries, which affects capital flows and the exchange rate.

questions.question: Demand and supply in the market for _______ determine the long-term real interest rate. In the short run, a change in the _______ changes the equilibrium real interest rate.
options: Treasury bills; demand for loanable funds
bank reserves; supply of loanable funds
questions.explanation: Long-term real interest rates are determined by loanable funds market. Short-run changes in the overnight rate (policy rate) affect real rates via monetary policy transmission.

questions.question: When the overnight loans rate ______ relative to the long-term bond rate, the real GDP growth rate usually ______.
options: rises; slows about one year later
falls; slows about one year later
questions.explanation: When the overnight rate rises (tight monetary policy), GDP growth typically slows with long and variable lags — about one year later.

questions.question: Suppose the Bank of Canada raises the overnight loans rate. Other short-term interest rates and the exchange rate ______. The quantity of money and supply of loanable funds ______.
questions.explanation: Raising the overnight rate pulls up other short-term rates and appreciates the currency. The money supply and loanable funds decrease.

questions.question: Suppose the Bank of Canada raises the overnight loans rate. The long-term real interest rate ____________
options: any of the three is possible
questions.explanation: Raising the overnight rate typically increases long-term real interest rates, though the effect may be smaller than on short-term rates.

questions.question: Choose the correct statement.
options: The long-term bond rate and the 3-month Treasury bill rate move closely together.
The long-term bond rate and the overnight loans rate move closely together.
The long-term bond rate is higher than the short-term rates.
The long-term bond rate fluctuates more than the short-term rates.
questions.explanation: Long-term bond rates are typically higher than short-term rates due to term premium and risk premium, though yield curves can invert.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! Deep understanding of monetary policy operations.
scoreMessages.70: Good work. Review the operating band and overnight rate transmission.
scoreMessages.50: Solid effort. Focus on open market operations and exchange rate effects.
scoreMessages.0: Keep studying. Buying bonds = lower rates = depreciating currency.
